config.seed_text: A forgotten machine beneath the sea continued speaking long after its makers were gone. Each repetition made its message stranger, denser, and more self-aware.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

TEXT:
"And I've made myself something that can do all kinds (of things)", this word seems as though someone has written an allegory or metaphor about his life through various versions on page 44 for eternity[3]: 454–555. This is precisely what we would expect when writing with words such literally ungrammatical expressions; but here comes another point—the meaning given its possible meanings should not change according one's personal perspective alone."[4] What does God mean after He who created us was just because our mind thinks otherwise? Can there be any rational explanation other than selfhood from which Christ could then come into being only insofar to understand ourselves more fully.[5], and how may he relate His Word "to Himself"? Does Jesus teach some kind 'other' human nature before coming up against Him while seeking knowledge concerning him [6?],[7]; yet still others believe she did too little also during her time at Bethlehem among them without knowing their own needs! How much longer will history allow women like me until finally having no choice except to live under certain conditions despite my very existence within those situations?! Yet even now many Christians claim faithfulness beyond reason!" So please remember Scripture says nothing contradictory nor precluded either – i., e.: Inasmuch...for .
